name: dialog
title: Dialog
description: A modal dialog with title, description, content area, and action footer, built on Perry sheet APIs.

docs.overview: Dialog displays a modal overlay using Perry's sheet APIs. Use DialogHeader for the title and description, DialogContent for the body, and DialogFooter for action buttons. Dialogs capture focus and block interaction with the rest of the application until dismissed.
docs.usage:
Dialog({
  open: true,
  onOpenChange: (v) => { isOpen = v },
  title: 'Confirm Action',
  description: 'Are you sure you want to proceed?',
  children: [DialogContent({ children: [Text('Additional details here.')] })],
})
docs.accessibility: Dialogs use the 'dialog' accessibility role. Ensure titles are descriptive and focus is trapped within the dialog while open. Provide a clear close or cancel action.

platformNotes.macos: Renders as a native AppKit sheet with modal behavior.
platformNotes.ios: Renders as a native UIKit sheet with modal behavior.
platformNotes.linux: Renders as a GTK modal dialog.
platformNotes.windows: Renders using the current Perry Windows backend modal behavior.
platformNotes.web: Renders as a centered overlay with backdrop.

props.name: open
props.description: Whether the dialog is currently visible

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Callback when dialog open state changes

props.name: description
props.description: Dialog description text below the title

examples.name: default
examples.title: Basic Dialog

examples.name: with-content
examples.title: Dialog with Custom Content

examples.name: with-footer
examples.title: Dialog with Custom Footer

examples.name: custom-size
examples.title: Custom Size Dialog
examples.code:
Dialog({
  open: true,
  title: 'Settings',
  description: 'Configure your application.',
  width: 500,
  height: 400,
  children: [
    DialogContent({ children: [Text('Settings content here.')] }),
  ],
  onOpenChange: (open) => { isOpen = open },
})

agent.summary: Dialog is a modal overlay for confirmations, forms, and focused user interactions that require attention before proceeding.
useWhen: Collecting user input in a focused context
Displaying critical information that requires acknowledgment
Showing forms or settings in a modal context
doNotUseWhen: Inline content that does not need to block interaction
layoutHints: Use DialogHeader for title and description at the top
Place primary action button on the right in the footer
Use destructive variant on confirm buttons for dangerous actions
Keep dialog content concise; use scroll for long content
Default width is 400px, adjust for complex forms
accessibilityChecklist: Provide a descriptive title for screen readers
Include a close or cancel action
Ensure focus is trapped within the dialog while open
Announce dialog opening to assistive technology
Return focus to triggering element on close
